text.title: Pain Mining and Niche Viability Scoring
text.summary: Pain point mining source strategy with search query patterns, and the 5-criteria weighted niche viability model with decision thresholds.

section.title: Pain Mining Sources and Queries

rule.statement: Mine pain points from at least 3 source types: High-signal sources (Reddit with "frustrating/hate/problem", Twitter/X with "{product} sucks", 1-3 star App Reviews) + Medium-signal sources (Product-specific forums, Quora "solution" searches) + Low-signal sources (LinkedIn industry discussions). Record source URL for every quote — verification is mandatory before any decision.
rule.rationale: High-signal sources capture authentic frustration; low-signal sources (LinkedIn) are filtered by professional presentation. Without source URLs, LLM-synthesized quotes cannot be verified and must be discarded.

section.p: Search query patterns: "{keyword} frustrating" site:reddit.com; "{competitor} alternative" site:reddit.com; "{product} review" 1-star; "I wish {tool} could"; "hate using {tool}"; "looking for {solution}".
Analysis process: collect 50+ mentions, categorize by theme, count frequency per theme, note intensity language ("I hate" vs. "a bit annoying"), identify gaps in current solutions.

rule.statement: Score each niche on five criteria: Market Size (25% weight, scale 1-10: <$10M=1-3, $10-100M=4-6, >$100M=7-10), Competition (20%: 10+ competitors=1-3, 3-10=4-6, <3=7-10), Barriers (20%: high capital/regulatory=1-3, medium=4-6, low=7-10), Profitability (20%: margins <20%=1-3, 20-40%=4-6, >40%=7-10), Your Fit (15%: no relevant skills=1-3, some=4-6, perfect match=7-10). Weighted score = sum of (score x weight).
rule.rationale: Market size and competition receive the highest weights because they are external constraints that cannot be changed; Your Fit receives the lowest weight because skills can be learned, but it remains non-zero because execution risk is real.

rule.statement: Apply decision thresholds: 7.5-10 = strong opportunity (proceed); 5.5-7.4 = proceed with caution; 3.5-5.4 = significant risks (major pivot needed); below 3.5 = pass.
rule.rationale: The thresholds are heuristic, not precise; they function as conversation starters that force explicit justification for each score, not as automatic decision rules.

example.note: AI Writing Tool niche: Market Size 8 ($200M), Competition 4 (crowded), Barriers 6 (ML expertise), Profitability 7 (SaaS), Your Fit 8 (ML background). Weighted: (8x0.25)+(4x0.2)+(6x0.2)+(7x0.2)+(8x0.15) = 2+0.8+1.2+1.4+1.2 = 6.6. Decision: Proceed with caution — differentiate from incumbents.

rule.statement: Never trust agent-generated search volume numbers without injecting real data; niche scoring requires explicit market size data or the agent anchors on vague impressions; G2 and Trustpilot block automated scraping — use cached Apify datasets.
rule.rationale: LLMs exhibit availability bias toward market sizes and problem frequencies common in training data; agent niche scores without real data are plausible-sounding fiction.

text.reference: LLM prompt to return raw niche scores as JSON
